definitions.name: endpoint_24_tk_quotient
definitions.text: The finite endpoint-24 quotient is the L8 T/K orbit set of the 236 endpoint-24 records; it has 59 size-four orbits and three affineness states.

definitions.name: b3_endpoint_selector
definitions.text: The B3 endpoint selector is selected_value_nonaffine, i.e. the L8 state selected_value_affine=false, selected_mask_affine=false, cell_value_affine=false.

definitions.name: transport_atom_payload
definitions.text: A transport atom payload is the Item7-H support-action atom attached to an endpoint orbit: support-action id, defect, signature, error directions, and relation code.

definitions.name: paper_local_endpoint_transport_theorem
definitions.text: A paper-local endpoint transport theorem may state the finite selector and payload inside the certified Endpoint-24 affine-defect atlas extension atlas, without claiming a symbolic endpoint classification or public record-level widening.

guardrails: Item7-J is paper-local finite packaging, not new symbolic mathematics.
Do not claim the selected_value_nonaffine orbit count is derived non-enumeratively.
Do not claim a non-enumerative endpoint-24 T/K quotient classification.
Do not claim public-release readiness or full non-affine endpoint-24 classification.

lemmas.name: endpoint_selector_exactness
lemmas.statement: In the L8 endpoint-24 T/K quotient, selected_value_nonaffine selects exactly 15 orbits and 60 records, with zero false positives, zero false negatives, and no partial orbit.

lemmas.name: carrier_atom_bijection
lemmas.statement: The 15 selected endpoint orbits are exactly the 15 Item7-H lower-geometry transport atoms, ordered as STEP6-SA01 through STEP6-SA15.

lemmas.name: transport_payload_consistency
lemmas.statement: The attached endpoint transport payload has defect split d4=8,d6=7 and signature multiplicities 1,1,1,2,2,1,4,3 across the eight B3 signatures.

lemmas.name: paper_boundary
lemmas.statement: The endpoint transport theorem is finite and paper-local: the symbolic endpoint count proof, non-enumerative T/K quotient derivation, and non-enumerative support/error payload attachment remain open.

proof_steps.claim: Use L8/Item7-I to fix the finite endpoint-24 T/K quotient and exact B3 endpoint selector.
Identify the selected endpoint orbits with the Item7-H lower-geometry transport atoms.
Attach the defect, signature, error-direction, and relation-code payload to each carrier orbit.
Preserve the finite paper-local boundary and keep symbolic endpoint debts open.
Export the theorem block, certificate rows, and guardrails into the final paper package.

summary.next_task: Create the standalone .tex export from PAPER_FINAL_THEOREM_B1_B2_B3.md plus the Item7-J endpoint transport package.

tex_export_checklist: Include Item7-I and Item7-J in the certificate/source table.
Keep the Nimm0 attribution caveat unless the bibliographic source is verified.
Keep public record-level scope, universal, full-classification, and symbolic endpoint-count claims out.

theorem.name: B3 endpoint transport theorem, paper-local finite form
not_claimed: a symbolic proof that selected_value_nonaffine must have exactly 15 T/K orbits
a non-enumerative derivation of the endpoint-24 T/K quotient
a non-enumerative attachment of the support/error payload
theorem.statement: Inside the certified Endpoint-24 affine-defect atlas endpoint-24 T/K quotient, an endpoint orbit is a B3 support-action carrier if and only if its selected values are non-affine. Equivalently, the exact endpoint selector is selected_value_nonaffine.  The selector has exactly 15 orbits and 60 records.  These 15 orbits are in canonical bijection with the Item7-H lower-geometry transport atoms, carrying the 15 support-action payloads STEP6-SA01 through STEP6-SA15.
